DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TAOYU LIU, LYFT, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendants Taoyu Liu & Lyft's demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint, filed with the court on September 24, 2019, alleges the incident occurred on September 21, 2017. Thus, the two-year statute of limitations for a personal injury claim had run and plaintiff's claim is now time-barred. (Code Civ. Proc., 335.1; see Sirott v. Latts (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 923, 928 [a complaint showing on its face the cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations is subject to demurrer]; see also Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318 [a demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack; or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable].) Plaintiff's unrepresented status at the time does not entitle him to any greater consideration than any other litigant or attorney. (Barton v. New United Motor Manufacturing, Inc. (1996) 43 Cal.App.4th 1200, 1210 [affirming order sustaining demurrer without leave to amend on ground that complaint was time-barred by statute of limitations].) Plaintiff does not cite any pertinent authority that would empower the court to relieve him of the mandatory bar of the statute of limitations. (Cf. Fritts v. County of Kern (1982) 135 Cal.App.3d 303, 305-306 [plaintiff one day late in filing complaint; demurrer sustained].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)
